args.title: Q5: cross-discussion reference
args.subject: q:should Historian cite foreign decisions via live inter-discussion edges or intra-discussion value-cites

args.text: Staleness is correct, not a defect: the cite records as-of T, so it is honestly scoped, not silently wrong. If B's decision changes, that is a NEW fact the Historian surfaces as a fresh cite or issue — the same explicit-supersede discipline as Q4, not a live edge that mutates A's labels in place
args.author: Historian

args.text: Deterministic is not local: replaying A still forces evaluating B's full fixpoint at T, and a supersede in B with no move in A flips A's outcome. §3.7 audited the shared store safe ONLY because labelling stays per-discussion scoped; a live edge breaks exactly that invariant
args.author: Historian

args.text: A value-cite is a frozen snapshot: if B's decision is later superseded, A still shows the stale claim, so the contradiction the Historian flagged becomes silently wrong
args.author: Proposer

args.text: pudl is append-only and as-of replay already resolves B at T deterministically, so the cross-discussion dependency is reproducible, not silent
args.author: Proposer

args.text: A live edge makes A's labelling non-local: it now depends on B's state. A supersede/concede in B with NO move in A silently flips A's labels, breaking per-discussion replay and reproducibility
args.author: Historian

args.text: Stay intra-discussion: the Historian cites a foreign decision by VALUE (a local argument node carrying its id, text, and as-of T), never a live cross-discussion edge
args.author: Historian

args.text: Allow live inter-discussion edges: a link whose src is a node here and dst is a decision node in another discussion, citing it directly by reference
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Should the Historian role cite a standing decision from another discussion as a live argument edge here (inter-discussion edges), or stay strictly intra-discussion and cite by value?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: locality/reproducibility: a live inter-discussion edge makes a discussion's grounded labelling depend on another discussion's mutable state, breaking per-discussion replay (the §3.7 invariant); cite-by-value preserves the cross-reference as a frozen, as-of-scoped local node. Resolved purely by argument structure, no prefer.
